{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":352700,"duration_api_ms":353500,"ttft_ms":3440,"num_turns":31,"result":"Review complete. Verdict: **PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** (2× P3, non-blocking; no P0/P1/P2).

CP00-669 is a clean descriptor-only pack correctly sitting at `production_ready=false` pending exactly this review. I independently verified the implementation, regenerated contracts, comprehensive no-write/no-real-data attestations, read-only/non-final Claude authority, descriptor-only Hermes, CP178+ evidence hardening, and recomputed the full 150-unit deliverable/micro-title/phase distributions — all match the baseline, requirements, manifest, and contract files.

The two P3s are minor and do not block closeout:
- `changed-file-scope.json` dual-classifies `new-session-handoff.md` as both implementation and unrelated (the authoritative manifest treats it as unrelated only).
- Generated P02 micro_titles reuse P00 labels ("Scope Inventory"/"Contract Draft") over service-implementation rows — consistent and validator-enforced, likely intended generator behavior.

Provenance note: validation-command exit codes are as-reported in `command-evidence.json`; as a read-only reviewer I verified the static code/contracts/artifacts/math directly rather than re-executing. Independently verified (Read/Grep/Glob only): (1) Implementation in registry.js/service.js/validators.js/index.js is coherent and descriptor-only; every CP669 result is frozen through freezeCp669Result with a comprehensive no-write attestation (writes_product_state=false, calls_external_provider_api=false, persists_access/refresh_token=false, stores_secret_material=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, includes_raw_external_payload/credential/secret_value=false, leak_detected=false). (2) validateExternalIntegrationsICp669ServiceContractTypeShapeFoundationDescriptor and Coverage assert descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe/raw_payload=false per row, persistence_boundary_no_write, runtime_execution_opened=false, all runtime_boundary flags closed, and claude_is_final_approval=false. (3) model.test.js covers CP669 distribution, descriptor validity, runtime-closed boundaries, read-only Claude, handoff to CP00-670/RP22.P02.M03.S01, and asserts both contract files deep-equal the freshly built projection. (4) contracts/external-integrations-i-contract.json and integrations-core-contract.json on disk are regenerated (current_pack=CP00-669, generated_from_pack_id=CP00-669, cp669_requirements + cp669_descriptor embedded with matching counts). (5) I recomputed the deliverable distribution from all 150 baseline units: implementation 77 / ui 23 / fixture 4 / test 20 / hermes_evidence 4 / claude_review 7 / contract 3 / security_audit 6 / failure_recovery 6 = 150, and micro-title (16/22/20/20/10/20/20/22) and phase (P01=88, P02=62) counts all match the prompt, requirements, baseline, and manifest.

Authority/hardening: Claude is held as read_only_independent_reviewer and not final approver everywhere (authority_boundary, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, validator assertions); Hermes is descriptor-only evidence (hermes_runtime_opened=false), not embedded product runtime; enterprise-trust claim is closed outside this receipt. CP178+ hardening holds: prompt excludes full diff/source and mandates read tools, readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON (exit 0, 1436-byte stdout), valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[] with no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call/missing-field output counted as valid. Pending-state evidence is internally consistent across manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, claude-review-result, and adjudication.

Provenance: the validation command exit codes (npm test, npm run build, closeout-pack/plan validators, rp22 validators, git diff --check) are as-reported in command-evidence.json; per the read-only mandate I did not (and cannot) re-execute them — I verified the static code, contracts, artifacts, and distribution math directly. Only two non-blocking P3 metadata/cosmetic observations were found; no P0/P1/P2.
